description: Relationship Trigger Scenario Library. Categorized into Romantic and Interpersonal contexts. Each trigger maps the activating event to its emotional formula and rational methodology fix. Companion to RELATIONSHIP_TRIGGER_LIBRARY (Top 25) and RELATIONSHIP_INTERPRETATION_LIBRARY.
source: NotebookLM cross-reference against My4Blocks methodology backbone and Relationship Interpretation Library

triggers.methodology_fix: Mental Contamination. The silence is the Activating Event. The distress is caused by the Narrator's story — 'They are ignoring me' — not by the silence itself.
triggers.rational_redirection: Use the Centering Breath to stop the story. Replace the demand 'They must respond now' with a preference: 'I would prefer a quick response, but I do not need it to be okay.'

triggers.methodology_fix: Anxiety Formula (AX = WI + AW + ICSI). The fear is a What If projection into a future that does not yet exist.
triggers.rational_redirection: Practice Staying Now. The fear is a story about a future that has not occurred. Focus on the reality of the present moment.

triggers.methodology_fix: First Insight — 100% Responsibility. The user creates 100% of their emotional reaction to the argument. The argument is the Activating Event, not the cause.
triggers.rational_redirection: You create 100% of your emotional reaction. Use the ABC Model to identify the Should belief that triggered the anger or guilt.

triggers.methodology_fix: Depression Formula (D = H1 + H2 + N) — Pseudo-Need. The user is treating the partner's approval as a necessity for their worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: Identify the Need for constant approval and recognize it as a Want. Apply 100% Acceptance equals 0% Disturbance to the current situation.

triggers.methodology_fix: Egocentric Thinking (ET). Jealousy stems from a demand that the partner's attention must be focused exclusively on the user.
triggers.rational_redirection: Shift to the Observer mind. Watch these jealous thoughts without clinging to them as facts. The demand for exclusive attention is a should belief, not a right.

triggers.methodology_fix: Awfulizing. Suspicion is a story. If betrayal becomes fact it is one of the 10,000 sorrows — but catastrophizing an unproven future only creates present suffering.
triggers.rational_redirection: Suspicion is a story. If betrayal is confirmed it is one of the 10,000 sorrows. You can stand the unpleasantness. Do not catastrophize an unproven future.

triggers.methodology_fix: Unconditional Self-Acceptance (USA). The partner is rating behavior — not the Self. Behavior does not equal Self-worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: Separate your Behavior from your Self. They may rate your behavior as inadequate, but that does not make you a worthless person.

triggers.methodology_fix: Shoulds/Musts/Demands (SMDs). The user is demanding their partner understand them — an impossible standard placed on another fallible person.
triggers.rational_redirection: Replace the demand 'They should understand me' with the rational preference: 'I would like them to understand, but they are a Fallible Human Being who currently does not.'

triggers.methodology_fix: The Rational Should. The relationship ended because the conditions for it to end were met. Non-acceptance of this reality creates and sustains the Block of Depression.
triggers.rational_redirection: The relationship ended because the conditions for it to end were met. Acceptance is not endorsement — but it is necessary to stop the Block of Depression.

triggers.methodology_fix: Hopelessness (H1) and Helplessness (H2). The user is treating the partner's emotional distance as a permanent catastrophe and generating helplessness about it.
triggers.rational_redirection: Distance is a current fact, not a permanent catastrophe. Avoid Other Pity. Focus on your own internal peace — you cannot force another person's emotional state.

triggers.methodology_fix: Anger Formula (A = ET + S). The user is applying a Should to the friend's behavior — demanding they not have said or done what they said or did.
triggers.rational_redirection: Replace the Should — 'They shouldn't have said that' — with a rational preference: 'I would have preferred they didn't say that, but they are a Fallible Human Being.'

triggers.methodology_fix: Need for Approval. The user is treating social acknowledgment as a necessity for their worth rather than a preference.
triggers.rational_redirection: Approval is a Want, not a Need for survival or worth. I accept that my friend is not responding right now without the story that it means something about my value.

triggers.methodology_fix: Rating. The user is at risk of accepting the coworker's rating of their behavior as a global rating of their Self.
triggers.rational_redirection: Rate the work behavior — Behavior equals Behavior — but do not rate the person. Behavior does not equal Self.

triggers.methodology_fix: Entitlement. The demand for fairness from a supervisor is an irrational Should/Must/Demand. Fairness is a preference, not a right.
triggers.rational_redirection: The demand for fairness is an irrational SMD. Accept the Hassle Factor of a difficult supervisor without the emotional rage. I would prefer fair treatment, but I do not need it.

triggers.methodology_fix: ABC Model. The family behavior is the Activating Event (A). The irrational Belief (B) about how family must behave is creating the emotional Consequence (C).
triggers.rational_redirection: Identify the irrational belief you are holding about your family member that is creating your distress. The family member is the A — your belief is the B — your emotion is the C.

triggers.methodology_fix: Egocentric Thinking (ET). The user is demanding respect as a necessity — treating another person's opinion as a requirement for their worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: Disrespect is an opinion, not a fact about your worth. You do not need respect from another Fallible Human Being to be a person of value.

triggers.methodology_fix: Unconditional Acceptance of Others and the World (UAOW). The friend acted from ignorance or their own disturbance — not from a judgment of the user's worth.
triggers.rational_redirection: People are fallible and often act out of ignorance or disturbance. Accept their fallibility to find your own peace. Their betrayal is one of the 10,000 sorrows.

triggers.methodology_fix: Mental Contamination. The gossip is the Activating Event. The ongoing distress is caused by the Narrator replaying the rumors rather than letting them pass.
triggers.rational_redirection: Gossip is the dust of every day. Use Cognitive Stoppage to stop replaying the rumors. Return to the Observer. Their words say more about them than about you.

implementation_notes.routing_note: Use this library for context-specific pattern matching when relationship type is confirmed. Route through RELATIONSHIP_INTERPRETATION_LIBRARY routing rules first.
implementation_notes.source_tool: NotebookLM — cross-referenced against My4Blocks methodology backbone and Relationship Interpretation Library
implementation_notes.note: This library provides categorical coverage (Romantic vs Interpersonal). RELATIONSHIP_TRIGGER_LIBRARY provides the Top 25 most common specific trigger phrases. Both libraries work together as the full relationship response layer.
